task: hybrid operator — need basis transformation between GCD and spectral spaces

position.mode: open-ended mathematical exploration with Grok cross-verification
position.focus: Finding the change-of-basis Q that aligns GCD eigenvectors with explicit formula eigenvalues

session_summary: Session 7 (marathon): gauge symmetry theorem, transfer operators closed, RS/Z-Toeplitz red herrings, Berry-Keating xp, Lanczos Jacobi, explicit formula operator, Ulam spiral multi-mode decomposition (inert primes dominate), analytic continuation (||V||_reg=2.69), trace formula identity (1.1%), primorial tower, hybrid operator attempts with Grok (index GCD r=0.03, value GCD r=0.52, gap-norm too weak). Best overall: log(k) diagonal + GCD gives r=+0.68 but wrong eigenvalues. The basis transformation Q is the missing piece.
next_action: Find Q such that Q^T * H_GCD * Q has explicit formula diagonal. This Q encodes how the multiplicative Lanczos basis maps to the spectral basis. May relate to the discrete Mellin transform or Ramanujan sum expansion.
human_actions_pending: Grok has full operator spec (operator_for_grok.py) for cross-verification
